description_html:
Case Mystery is a high-intensity business case competition by the Pivot Consulting Club that challenges participants to think like consultants, founders, and business leaders. Teams will analyze real-world business problems and propose structured, practical solutions across domains such as entrepreneurship, strategy, marketing, finance, operations, and market entry.

Eligibility:

  • Open to undergraduate and postgraduate students from all recognized colleges and universities.
  • Students from all disciplines and specializations are eligible.
  • Inter-college teams are not allowed.
  • Inter-specialization teams are allowed.

Round 1 – Case Screening (Elimination Round):

  • Mode: Online.
  • Case Release:
  • Submission Deadline:
  • Teams will receive one business problem statement based on real-world entrepreneurial and business scenarios.
  • Teams must analyze the case and submit a brief solution deck or written response in the format specified by the organizers on the Unstop platform.

Round 2 – Grand Finale (On-Campus):

  • Mode: On-campus.
  • Shortlisted teams will be given one final case.
  • Teams will present their solutions before a panel of judges, followed by a Q&A session to assess clarity of thought, depth of analysis, and practicality of recommendations.

Team Composition:

  • Teams of 2–4 members.
  • Each participant may be part of only one team.
  • Team members cannot be changed after registration.

Competition Format & Process:

  • The competition will be conducted in two rounds.

Evaluation Criteria (Round 1):

  • Problem understanding.
  • Structure of approach.
  • Logic and analytical depth.
  • Feasibility and innovation.
  • Clarity and coherence of presentation.

Qualification:

  • Based on submissions, top-performing teams will advance to the Grand Finale (Round 2).

Evaluation Criteria (Round 2):

  • Strategic depth and problem structuring.
  • Data-backed reasoning and insights.
  • Practicality and impact of solutions.
  • Creativity and originality of ideas.
  • Presentation skills and ability to handle Q&A.

Rules & Guidelines:

  • The decision of the judges and organizing committee shall be final and binding.
  • Only original work is allowed; any form of plagiarism, copied content, or misrepresentation will lead to immediate disqualification.
  • Use of unfair means, impersonation, or external assistance is strictly prohibited.
  • Teams must strictly follow the submission format, deadlines, and file guidelines provided by the organizers on Unstop. Late submissions will not be accepted.
  • During the final presentation, teams must adhere to the allotted time limits and maintain professional conduct.
  • In case of a tie, the judges may conduct an additional Q&A or assign a tie-breaker task at their discretion.
  • The organizers reserve the right to modify the format, timeline, or evaluation criteria if required for the smooth conduct of the event.
